initiatives.title: Wulver A100 GPU research cluster
initiatives.description: NJIT documents Wulver as a 127-node research cluster with 100 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 8 TB GPU RAM, 1 PB storage, and MIG partitioning for efficient shared GPU use.
initiatives.significance: This closes NJIT’s compute-capacity gap with a precise public GPU count.

research_centers.name: AI research or coordination activity
research_centers.focus: Public AI research, teaching, or tool coordination evidenced in cited sources.

computing_infrastructure.summary: NJIT’s Wulver cluster entered production in early 2024 with 127 total nodes. Public HPC documentation lists 25 GPU nodes with 4 NVIDIA A100 80GB GPUs each, 100 total GPUs, 8 TB total GPU RAM, 1 PB storage, and MIG support for partitioning A100 GPUs for shared workloads including LLM fine-tuning and PyTorch training.

regulatory_context.summary: State and system AI context checked only at high level; institution-level sources carry the profile.

narrative_summary: New Jersey Institute of Technology now has a precise public AI-compute signal through Wulver. The early-2024 cluster has 127 nodes, 100 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 8 TB of GPU memory, 1 PB storage, and MIG partitioning that supports LLM fine-tuning, PyTorch, and other shared GPU workloads. Existing profile evidence still covers the Center for AI Research, the M.S. in Artificial Intelligence, AAC&U AI pedagogy participation, and the Grace Hopper biomedicine fellowship. Remaining gaps are mainly enterprise AI platform contracts or user counts, AI budget totals outside Wulver and small fellowships, and student subgroup outcome metrics.
